success_surface: Hybrid pandas/openpyxl workflow for complex cell-level calculations with precise output positioning
effective_behaviors: Combined pandas for bulk data processing with openpyxl for targeted cell writes
Programmatic column range extraction from DataFrame indices based on instruction scope
Custom function creation for non-standard cell-level logic requiring iteration
Excel cell address conversion using library utilities instead of hardcoded positions
Output placement aligned precisely with specified answer_position without altering other cells
Single-pass save operation to maintain existing workbook structure and formatting
evidence: df = pd.read_excel(...) for data loading, wb = load_workbook(...) for write operations
cols_to_process = df.columns[1:5] extracted columns B-E dynamically from position range
def calculate_consecutive_ones(series): custom loop-based calculation for consecutive value groups
col_letter = get_column_letter(i + 2) converted numeric index to Excel column letter
ws[f"{col_letter}15"].value = total wrote only to B15:E15 range as specified
wb.save(output_path) single save maintained all pre-existing sheet content and style

memory_items.title: Hybrid Tool Strategy for Cell-Level Tasks
memory_items.lesson: Use pandas for data computation and openpyxl for targeted cell writes when formatting or workbook structure must be preserved
memory_items.generalization: When cell-level output is required, leverage pandas for efficient columnar data processing but retain openpyxl Workbook objects for precise cell address writing to avoid reformatting risks from pandas.to_excel()

memory_items.title: Position-Aware Column Selection
memory_items.lesson: Derive target column indices from answer_position specifications using DataFrame indexing rather than hardcoding column names or numbers
memory_items.generalization: Parse answer_position to determine column row ranges, then select corresponding DataFrame columns by positional index to ensure alignment with the expected output region

memory_items.title: Custom Logic Functions for Complex Patterns
memory_items.lesson: Implement iterative helper functions in Python for calculations involving consecutive values, running totals, or pattern detection that lack native pandas one-liners
memory_items.generalization: When built-in aggregation functions cannot express the required logic (e.g., consecutive sequences, conditional groupings), encapsulate the algorithm in a dedicated function and apply via map or comprehension

memory_items.title: Dynamic Cell Address Generation
memory_items.lesson: Convert programmatic column/row indices to Excel-style cell references using library utilities rather than string concatenation
memory_items.generalization: Always use openpyxl.utils.get_column_letter() for column index-to-letter conversion when targeting specific cell addresses, ensuring compatibility with any column width in the workbook

edits.content:
## Trace-Derived Lessons

### A. Hybrid Tool Strategy for Cell-Level Tasks
- Use pandas for bulk data computation, openpyxl for targeted cell writes when workbook formatting must be retained.
- Load workbook with openpyxl, read data with pandas, write specific cells via openpyxl worksheet object to preserve sheet structure.

### B. Position-Aware Column Selection
- Derive target column indices from answer_position specifications using DataFrame positional indexing.
- Match column selection to the requested output range rather than relying on hardcoded headers or fixed positions.

### C. Custom Logic Functions for Complex Patterns
- Encapsulate non-standard calculations (consecutive sequences, conditional groups) in Python helper functions.
- Apply custom functions across columns using list comprehensions or iterrows for flexible pattern-based logic.

### D. Dynamic Cell Address Generation
- Use openpyxl.utils.get_column_letter() for converting numeric column indices to Excel-style references.
- Combine column letters with row numbers dynamically to target exact cells without manual address construction.
